children.heading: Armed Forces
children.label: 1 Armed Forces
children.text: The term “Armed Forces” means the Army, Navy, Air Force, Marine Corps, and Coast Guard.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate current expenditures of all local educational agencies in the State; divided by

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of children in average daily attendance for whom such agencies provided free public education.

children.label: A
children.text: the preparation of drawings and specifications for school facilities;

children.label: B
children.text: erecting, building, acquiring, altering, remodeling, repairing, or extending school facilities;

children.label: C
children.text: inspecting and supervising the construction of school facilities; and

children.label: D
children.text: debt service for such activities.

children.heading: Current expenditures
children.label: 4 Current expenditures
children.text: The term “current expenditures” means expenditures for free public education, including expenditures for administration, instruction, attendance and health services, pupil transportation services, operation and maintenance of plant, fixed charges, and net expenditures to cover deficits for food services and student body activities, but does not include expenditures for community services, capital outlay, and debt service, or any expenditures made from funds awarded under part A of subchapter I. The determination of whether an expenditure for the replacement of equipment is considered a current expenditure or a capital outlay shall be determined in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles as determined by the State.

children.label: i
children.text: owned by the United States or leased by the United States from another entity;

children.label: I
children.text: held in trust by the United States for individual Indians or Indian tribes;

children.label: II
children.text: held by individual Indians or Indian tribes subject to restrictions on alienation imposed by the United States;

children.label: aa
children.text: that is not taxed is, for the purposes of this paragraph, considered tax-exempt due to Federal law; and

children.label: bb
children.text: is considered Federal property for the purpose of this paragraph if the property is located within a Regional Educational Attendance Area that has no taxing power;

children.label: IV
children.text: public land owned by the United States that is designated for the sole use and benefit of individual Indians or Indian tribes; or

children.label: V
children.text: used for low-rent housing, as described in paragraph (10), that is located on land described in subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV) of this clause or on land that met one of those descriptions immediately before such property’s use for such housing;

children.label: I
children.text: part of a low-rent housing project assisted under the United States Housing Act of 1937 [ et seq.];42 U.S.C. 1437

children.label: II
children.text: used to provide housing for homeless children at closed military installations pursuant to ; orsection 11411 of title 42

children.label: III
children.text: used for affordable housing assisted under the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 ( et seq.); or25 U.S.C. 4101

children.label: iv
children.text: owned by a foreign government or by an international organization.

children.heading: Schools providing flight training to members of Air Force
children.label: B Schools providing flight training to members of Air Force
children.text: The term “Federal property” includes, so long as not subject to taxation by any State or any political subdivision of a State, and whether or not that tax exemption is due to Federal agreement, law, or policy, any school providing flight training to members of the Air Force under contract with the Air Force at an airport owned by a State or political subdivision of a State.

children.heading: Non-Federal easements, leases, licenses, permits, improvements, and certain other real property
children.label: C Non-Federal easements, leases, licenses, permits, improvements, and certain other real property
i
children.text: any non-Federal easement, lease, license, permit, or other such interest in Federal property as otherwise described in this paragraph, but not including any non-Federal fee-simple interest;

children.label: ii
children.text: any improvement on Federal property as otherwise described in this paragraph; and

children.label: iii
children.text: real property that, immediately before its sale or transfer to a non-Federal party, was owned by the United States and otherwise qualified as Federal property described in this paragraph, but only for one year beyond the end of the fiscal year of such sale or transfer.

children.heading: Certain Postal Service property and pipelines and utility lines
children.label: D Certain Postal Service property and pipelines and utility lines
i
children.text: any real property under the jurisdiction of the United States Postal Service that is used primarily for the provision of postal services; or

children.label: ii
children.text: pipelines and utility lines.

children.heading: Property with respect to which State or local tax revenues may not be expended, allocated, or available for free public education
children.label: E Property with respect to which State or local tax revenues may not be expended, allocated, or available for free public education
i
children.text: no tax revenues of the State or of any political subdivision of the State may be expended for the free public education of children who reside on that Federal property; or

children.label: ii
children.text: no tax revenues of the State are allocated or available for the free public education of such children.

children.heading: Property located in the State of Oklahoma owned by Indian housing authority for low-income housing
children.label: F Property located in the State of Oklahoma owned by Indian housing authority for low-income housing
i
children.text: is owned by an Indian housing authority and used for low-income housing (including housing assisted under or authorized by the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 [ et seq.]); and25 U.S.C. 4101

children.label: I
children.text: was designated by treaty as tribal land; or

children.label: II
children.text: satisfied the definition of Federal property under (Public Law 874, 81st Congress) (as such Act was in effect on the day preceding ).section 403(1)(A) of the Act of September 30, 1950October 20, 1994

children.label: A
children.text: at public expense, under public supervision and direction, and without tuition charge; and

children.label: i
children.text: includes preschool education; and

children.label: ii
children.text: does not include any education provided beyond grade 12.

children.heading: Indian lands
children.label: 7 Indian lands
children.text: The term “Indian lands” means any Federal property described in paragraph (5)(A)(ii) or (5)(F).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “local contribution percentage” means the percentage of current expenditures in the State derived from local and intermediate sources, as reported to and verified by the National Center for Education Statistics.

children.heading: Hawaii and District of Columbia
children.label: B Hawaii and District of Columbia
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), the local contribution percentage for Hawaii and for the District of Columbia shall be the average local contribution percentage for the 50 States and the District of Columbia.

children.label: i
children.text: means a board of education or other legally constituted local school authority having administrative control and direction of free public education in a county, township, independent school district, or other school district; and

children.label: ii
children.text: includes any State agency that directly operates and maintains facilities for providing free public education.

children.label: i
children.text: was constituted or reconstituted primarily for the purpose of receiving assistance under this subchapter or the Act of (Public Law 874, 81st Congress) (as such Act was in effect on the day preceding ) or increasing the amount of such assistance; orSeptember 30, 1950October 20, 1994

children.label: ii
children.text: is not constituted or reconstituted for legitimate educational purposes.

children.heading: Low-rent housing
children.label: 10 Low-rent housing
children.text: The term “low-rent housing” means housing located on property that is described in paragraph (5)(A)(iii).

children.label: A
children.text: the concurrent installation of equipment; and

children.label: B
children.text: the complete or partial replacement of an existing school facility, but only if such replacement is less expensive and more cost-effective than repair, renovation, or alteration of the school facility.

children.label: A
children.text: revenue produced within the boundaries of a local educational agency and available to such agency for such agency’s use; or

children.label: B
children.text: funds collected by another governmental unit, but distributed back to a local educational agency in the same proportion as such funds were collected as a local revenue source.

children.label: A
children.text: classrooms and related facilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: equipment, machinery, and utilities necessary or appropriate for school purposes.
